title: GDS 2.0 · Stable web components for everyone
content:
## Two years in the making

Green Core 2.0 marks the first stable, production-ready release of the web component layer. Starting from the experimental 1.x series, the team has rebuilt the internal rendering pipeline, unified event handling across all interactive components, and shipped a complete Angular wrapper library via auto-generation.

### What changed from 1.x

The most impactful change for consumers is the new property-based API. Where 1.x relied heavily on attributes and custom events with inconsistent naming, 2.0 introduces a consistent `onChange` / `onInput` convention and typed property access through the generated React and Angular wrappers.

### Angular wrappers

Every component in the library now has a corresponding Angular wrapper in `@sebgroup/green-core-ng`, auto-generated from the custom elements manifest. This means Angular teams can consume the design system with full type safety and change detection integration.

### Upgrade path

A dedicated migration guide is available in the documentation. For most projects, the migration is straightforward — the most common change is updating event listener syntax and switching to the new token names.
